engineGuide.h2: Peugeot 405 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot 405 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: The workhorse diesel that powered most UK 405s; indirect injection, robust block, but injection pump and head gasket issues drive replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: GL 1.9 D, SRDT 1.9 TD, GLX 1.9 D, Estate 1.9 D, Estate 1.9 TD, 4x4 Dangel 1.9 D
commonFailures: Injection pump wear causing hard starting (80k+ miles)
Timing belt snap causing valve damage
entries.cta: Get quotes for XUD9 Peugeot 405 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel Turbo
entries.history: Turbocharged evolution of the XUD9, adding 23hp for SRDT models; turbo oil seals and intercooler pipes are common failure points.
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal leak causing blue smoke
EGR valve clogging on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for XUD9TE Peugeot 405 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Late-production XUD variant with minor emissions updates; mechanically similar to XUD9 but with revised injection calibration.
commonFailures: Valve stem seal wear causing oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for XUD9A Peugeot 405 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The entry-level petrol unit for early 405 GL models; carburetted simplicity but prone to head gasket and timing belt issues.
commonFailures: Timing belt snap causing valve damage
entries.cta: Get quotes for XU5 Peugeot 405 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Mid-range petrol engine with fuel injection; better torque than XU5 but shares the same head gasket vulnerability on high-mileage examples.
commonFailures: Valve stem seal wear causing blue smoke
entries.cta: Get quotes for XU7 Peugeot 405 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Petrol
entries.history: Larger-capacity XU variant for STI models; more torque but same fundamental design, so head gasket and valve seal issues persist.
commonFailures: Valve stem seal wear on deceleration
Oil leak from rear crank seal
entries.cta: Get quotes for XU9 Peugeot 405 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Petrol 16v (Mi16)
entries.history: The legendary Mi16 engine; DOHC 16-valve design delivering 160hp, but complex valve train and high-revving nature increase rebuild costs.
commonFailures: Valve guide wear causing oil consumption
Head gasket failure under hard use
entries.cta: Get quotes for XU9J4 Peugeot 405 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Petrol 8v (GTI)
entries.history: The GTI's 8-valve XU9 variant; simpler than the Mi16 but still prone to classic XU weaknesses, especially on neglected examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for XU9J2 Peugeot 405 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The smallest petrol option for late-model GL and Estate variants; simple carburetted design but valve seal issues common on high mileage.
commonFailures: Valve stem seal failure causing blue smoke
Timing belt wear on neglected units
entries.cta: Get quotes for TU3 Peugeot 405 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 405 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Peugeot 405 Head Gasket Failure - Why XU Petrol Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 405 GL 1.6, SR 1.8, STI 1.9, Mi16 (1987-1997, XU5/XU7/XU9/XU9J4)
problems.rootCause: The XU engine's aluminium cylinder head expands differently to the cast-iron block under heat, stressing the head gasket; overheating from coolant leaks or failed thermostats accelerates failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace head gasket, reseal covers, flush cooling system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before warpage; not suitable if block/head damaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, machine surface, replace valves/guides, new gasket set
repairOptions.longevity: Good for moderate damage; requires specialist machining; 40-60k mile expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded gaskets, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage or severely damaged units; like-new reliability

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot 405 worth £1,500-£4,000, a full rebuilt engine often represents better long-term value than repeated head gasket repairs. We recommend replacement if the block shows signs of warpage or if the car has exceeded 120k miles.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt XU engine prices for your Peugeot 405

problems.h4: Peugeot 405 Valve Stem Seal Failure - Blue Smoke on Deceleration
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 405 GL, SR, STI, Mi16 (1987-1997, XU5/XU7/XU9/XU9J4)
problems.rootCause: Hardened rubber valve stem seals shrink with age, allowing oil to seep into combustion chambers; symptoms include blue smoke on overrun and increased oil consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cam cover, replace valve stem seals in situ
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix for early symptoms; may not address worn guides; 20-40k mile expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace seals, guides, valves, machine surface
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive fix for moderate wear; requires specialist; 60-80k mile expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new seals, guides, gaskets, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage units or when guides are worn beyond repair

problems.recommendation: If blue smoke is mild and oil consumption is under 1L per 1,000 miles, seal replacement may suffice. For heavy smoke or high-mileage engines, a rebuilt unit offers better reliability and value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 405 XU engine rebuild

problems.h4: Peugeot 405 Injection Pump Failure - XUD Diesel Won't Start
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 405 GL 1.9 D, SRDT 1.9 TD, GLX 1.9 D (1987-1997, XUD9/XUD9TE/XUD9A)
problems.rootCause: The Lucas/CAV rotary injection pump wears internally; symptoms include hard starting, rough idle, loss of power, and metallic knocking; water contamination accelerates failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove pump, bench-test, replace worn plungers/seals, recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if pump housing is sound; requires specialist diesel technician; 40-70k mile expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned exchange pump, bleed fuel system, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Good option if engine block is healthy; faster turnaround than full rebuild

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new/reconditioned pump, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when pump failure coincides with other age-related issues

problems.recommendation: XUD engines are robust; if the block is sound and compression is good, a pump recondition or exchange is often the most cost-effective solution. Only opt for full replacement if other major components show wear.
problems.cta: Compare XUD diesel engine prices for your Peugeot 405

problems.h4: Peugeot 405 Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Valve Damage
problems.affectedModels: All Peugeot 405 variants with XU/XUD/TU engines (1987-1997)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-100,000 miles if not replaced on schedule
problems.rootCause: Rubber timing belts degrade with age and heat; if not replaced every 48k miles/4 years, they can snap, causing pistons to strike valves in these interference engines.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, water pump, seals
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure; 48k mile/4 year interval

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, guides, machine surface, new gasket
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block/pistons undamaged; requires specialist assessment

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new belt kit, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option when valve damage is severe or block is scored; guaranteed reliability

problems.recommendation: Prevention is far cheaper than cure. If your belt is overdue, replace it immediately. If it has snapped, get a compression test first; if damage is extensive, a rebuilt engine is often more economical than head repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 405 timing belt replacement or engine rebuild

problems.rootCause: Age-related degradation of plastic coolant pipes, water pump impeller wear, thermostat sticking, and radiator core corrosion lead to coolant loss and overheating, which then triggers head gasket failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace water pump, thermostat, hoses, flush system, new coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative maintenance; addresses early overheating; 40-60k mile expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace head gasket, machine head, renew all cooling components
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if overheating caused minor gasket failure; requires specialist assessment

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for severe overheating damage or high-mileage engines; like-new reliability

problems.recommendation: Address cooling issues immediately. If overheating has occurred, get a compression test and oil inspection for coolant contamination. For engines over 120k miles, a rebuilt unit often offers better long-term value than repeated cooling repairs.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot 405 engine replacement prices after overheating

problems.h4: Peugeot 405 ECU & Electrical Faults - Erratic Running
problems.rootCause: The Bosch Motronic or Lucas injection ECU can suffer moisture damage from leaking scuttle panels or aged wiring looms, causing erratic idle, stalling, or no-start conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove ECU, clean connectors, repair damaged traces, reprogram
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for moisture damage only; requires automotive electronics specialist

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace aged wiring sections, connectors, relays, fuse box
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause of electrical faults; labour-intensive but cost-effective

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with reconditioned ECU, new loom sections, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best when electrical faults coincide with mechanical wear; guaranteed compatibility

problems.recommendation: Start with ECU diagnostics. If the ECU is faulty but the engine is mechanically sound, repair or exchange the ECU. Only consider engine replacement if mechanical issues are also present.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 405 fuel-injected engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 405
finalCta.paragraph: From head gasket leaks to injection pump wear, the Peugeot 405's common engine failures are well-documented and solvable. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Peugeot 405 specialists for rebuilt, reconditioned, or used engines. Every unit includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 405 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 405 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Peugeot 405 Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Peugeot 405 engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Peugeot 405 Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Professionally cleaned, inspected and rebuilt with new gaskets, seals and bearings to restore factory performance.
types.frontDescription: Professionally cleaned, inspected and rebuilt with new gaskets, seals and bearings to restore factory performance.
types.frontDisclaimer: Prices vary by engine code and supplier; warranty terms apply.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned Peugeot 405 engines undergo a full strip, clean and rebuild with new wear components. Ideal for XUD diesel and XU petrol units with moderate mileage. Includes 12-month warranty.
backBullets: Best for: Engines with sound blocks but worn internals
Typical turnaround: 3-5 working days from UK specialists

types.title: Peugeot 405 Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Comprehensive rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points like head gaskets and valve seals.
types.frontDescription: Comprehensive rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points like head gaskets and valve seals.
types.frontDisclaimer: Mi16 and turbo diesel units command premium pricing; confirm specs with supplier.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt Peugeot 405 engines feature upgraded gaskets, seals, bearings and often revised components to prevent repeat failures. The preferred choice for high-mileage or performance variants.
backBullets: Best for: High-mileage units or known weak points (XU head gaskets)
Includes: Bench testing, compression report, warranty documentation
Warranty: Peugeotmum 12-month unlimited mileage as standard

types.title: Peugeot 405 Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage donor engines from accident-damaged 405s; cost-effective but verify compression and service history.
types.frontDescription: Low-mileage donor engines from accident-damaged 405s; cost-effective but verify compression and service history.
types.frontDisclaimer: Mileage and condition vary; request compression test results before purchase.
types.backDescription: Used Peugeot 405 engines offer budget replacement from verified breakers. Always request compression figures, service history and a short warranty. Ideal for short-term ownership.
backBullets: Best for: Budget repairs or short-term vehicle retention
Verify: Compression test, oil pressure, service records
Warranty: Typically 30-90 days from breaker; confirm terms

types.title: Peugeot 405 Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Factory-spec rebuild to original tolerances with new or reconditioned components; closest to new-engine reliability.
types.frontDescription: Factory-spec rebuild to original tolerances with new or reconditioned components; closest to new-engine reliability.
types.frontDisclaimer: Remanufactured units are specialist-order; lead times may apply for Mi16/XUD9TE.
types.backDescription: Remanufactured Peugeot 405 engines are rebuilt to factory specifications using new or reconditioned components. The premium choice for enthusiasts and long-term ownership.
backBullets: Best for: Mi16, GTI or fleet vehicles requiring maximum reliability
Process: Full machining, new bearings, seals, gaskets, balancing
Warranty: Often 24-month cover available; confirm with supplier

types.title: Peugeot 405 Refurbished Engines
types.description: Light refurbishment addressing specific faults like head gasket or seals; cost-effective for targeted repairs.
types.frontDescription: Light refurbishment addressing specific faults like head gasket or seals; cost-effective for targeted repairs.
types.frontDisclaimer: Refurbishment scope varies; confirm exactly what is included before ordering.
types.backDescription: Refurbished Peugeot 405 engines address specific known issues like head gaskets or valve seals without a full rebuild. A middle-ground option for targeted repairs.
backBullets: Best for: Engines with one known fault and otherwise good condition
Scope: Typically head work, seal replacement, gasket refresh
Warranty: Usually 6-12 months on refurbished components only

types.title: Peugeot 405 Supply & Fit Engines

engineSizes.h2: Peugeot 405 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: Peugeot 405 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L XUD diesel was the backbone of the 405 range, fitted to GL, SRDT and GLX variants across both MK1 and MK2 generations. Known for robust construction but vulnerable to injection pump wear and head gasket issues on high-mileage examples.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 405 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 405 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.4L TU3 petrol powered late-model GL and Estate variants. Simple carburetted design but prone to valve stem seal wear on high mileage, causing blue smoke on deceleration.

items.title: Peugeot 405 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L XU5 petrol was the mainstream choice for early GL and GR models. Carburetted simplicity but shares the XU family's head gasket vulnerability and timing belt sensitivity.

items.title: Peugeot 405 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L XU7 petrol offered more torque for SR and Estate variants. Fuel-injected refinement but still susceptible to classic XU weaknesses like head gasket and valve seal issues.

items.title: Peugeot 405 1.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.9L XU9 petrol powered STI and early GTI models. More torque than the 1.8 but same fundamental design, so head gasket and valve seal issues persist on high-mileage examples.

items.title: Peugeot 405 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L XU10 was a late addition for certain export markets and special editions. Shares XU architecture but with revised internals; head gasket and timing belt remain key maintenance items. Not sure which engine size your Peugeot 405 needs? engineYears.h2: Peugeot 405 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 405's 1987-1997 UK production, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: MK1 Launch: XU Petrol & XUD Diesel Debut
years.description: The original 405 launched with carburetted XU petrol and indirect-injection XUD diesel engines, establishing the reliability profile that would define replacement demand for a decade.
keyChanges: MK1 saloon launched with GL, SR, STI and Mi16 trims
XU5 (1.6L), XU9 (1.9L) petrol and XUD9 (1.9L) diesel introduced
Mi16 variant debuts with high-performance XU9J4 16v engine
knownFor: Robust XUD diesel block but injection pump wear on high mileage
XU petrol head gasket vulnerability if cooling system neglected
Mi16's complex 16v engine commands premium rebuild costs
years.cta: Get quotes for 1987-1992 Peugeot 405 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 405 Mi16 engine replacement • Peugeot 405 XUD9 injection pump failure • Peugeot 405 XU head gasket cost

years.preview: MK2 Facelift: Fuel Injection & Turbo Diesel Arrive
years.description: The MK2 facelift introduced fuel injection across petrol ranges and the turbocharged XUD9TE diesel, improving refinement but adding new failure points like ECU moisture damage.
keyChanges: MK2 facelift with updated styling and interior
Fuel injection replaces carburettors on XU5M, XU7, XU9J2
XUD9TE turbo diesel introduced for SRDT variant
Estate (Break) body style expands variant range
knownFor: Fuel injection improves cold starting but ECU moisture damage risk
XUD9TE turbo adds performance but oil seal leaks common
Estate variants popular for high-mileage diesel use

years.preview: MK2 Late Production: Final Updates & Run-Out
years.description: Final production years saw minor emissions updates to XUD9A diesel and TU3 petrol, with replacement demand now driven by age-related failures rather than design flaws.
keyChanges: XUD9A diesel replaces XUD9 for GLX variants (emissions update)
TU3 1.4L petrol introduced for entry-level GL and Estate
Final run-out of Mi16 and GTI performance variants
Production ends in 1997 after 1.6 million units globally
knownFor: Late-model XUD9A more refined but same core reliability profile
TU3 1.4L simple but valve seal wear on high mileage
Collector interest in final-year Mi16/GTI drives premium rebuild demand

faq.h2: Peugeot 405 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 405 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Peugeot 405 engine typically costs £750-£2,400 supply-only, depending on variant and engine code. Supply & fit adds £450-£850 labour. Mi16 and turbo diesel units command premium pricing.
keyPoints: XUD9 diesel rebuilt: £850-£1,850 supply-only
XU petrol rebuilt: £750-£2,100 depending on size
Mi16 XU9J4 rebuilt: £2,200-£4,200 due to complexity
Supply & fit labour typically adds £450-£850
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 405 engine replacement prices now

items.question: What is the most reliable Peugeot 405 engine?
items.answer: The XUD9 1.9L diesel is widely regarded as the most robust Peugeot 405 engine when maintained, though injection pump wear is common on high-mileage examples. Petrol XU engines are smooth but more prone to head gasket issues.
keyPoints: XUD9 diesel: robust block, watch injection pump and head gasket
XU petrol: smooth but strict timing belt/cooling maintenance essential
Mi16 XU9J4: high-performance but complex, costly to rebuild
TU3 1.4L: simple but valve seal wear on high mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable Peugeot 405 diesel engine replacement

items.question: What causes Peugeot 405 head gasket failure?
items.answer: Peugeot 405 XU petrol engines suffer head gasket failure due to aluminium head/cast-iron block expansion differences under heat; overheating from coolant leaks or failed thermostats accelerates the issue.
keyPoints: XU engines: aluminium head expands differently to cast-iron block
Overheating from coolant leaks, failed thermostat or water pump
Symptoms: white smoke, coolant loss, oil contamination
Prevention: replace timing belt/water pump every 48k miles
items.cta: Compare rebuilt XU engine prices for your Peugeot 405

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Peugeot 405 engine?
items.answer: Yes, if the car is otherwise sound and you plan to keep it. A rebuilt Peugeot 405 engine (£750-£2,400) often costs less than the car's value and restores reliability for years of further use.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost: £750-£2,400 vs car value £1,500-£4,000
XUD diesel engines especially worth rebuilding for high-mileage users
Mi16 enthusiasts often invest in premium rebuilds for performance retention
Consider supply & fit cost (£450-£850) if lacking workshop facilities
items.cta: Find out if your Peugeot 405 is worth an engine replacement

items.question: What engine codes fit the Peugeot 405?
items.answer: Key Peugeot 405 engine codes include XUD9/XUD9TE (1.9L diesel), XU5/XU7/XU9 (1.6-1.9L petrol), XU9J4 (Mi16 16v), and TU3 (1.4L petrol). Always verify your exact code via DVLA lookup before ordering.
keyPoints: Diesel: XUD9, XUD9TE, XUD9A, DJY
Petrol: XU5, XU5M, XU7, XU9, XU9J2, XU9J4, TU3
Mi16 exclusive: XU9J4 16v
Use registration lookup to confirm exact code for your variant
items.cta: Get quotes for your specific Peugeot 405 engine code

items.question: How long does a Peugeot 405 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engine delivery typically takes 3-5 working days from UK specialists. Supply & fit installation adds 1-2 days labour, depending on garage workload and variant complexity.
keyPoints: Engine sourcing: 3-5 working days typical for common codes
XUD9/XU7 widely available; Mi16 XU9J4 may require longer lead time
Installation: 1-2 days labour for experienced Peugeot specialists
Allow extra time for ancillary parts (belts, hoses, mounts) if needed
items.cta: Check live Peugeot 405 engine availability and lead times

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Peugeot 405?
items.answer: While engine swaps are technically possible, we recommend sticking to factory-fitted codes (XUD, XU, TU) for the Peugeot 405 to ensure compatibility with electronics, mounts and emissions compliance.
keyPoints: Stick to factory codes: XUD9, XU7, XU9J4 etc. for plug-and-play fitment
Cross-model swaps (e.g., 306 engine) require custom mounts/wiring
Emissions and MOT compliance may be affected by non-standard swaps
Our network supplies only verified compatible engines for your variant
items.cta: Compare compatible Peugeot 405 engine replacement options

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement Peugeot 405 engine?
items.answer: Every rebuilt or reconditioned Peugeot 405 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Used engines typically carry shorter 30-90 day breaker warranties.
keyPoints: Rebuilt/recon engines: Peugeotmum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Used engines: typically 30-90 days from breaker; confirm terms
Warranty covers manufacturing defects, not installation errors
Always request written warranty documentation before purchase

items.question: How do I find my Peugeot 405 engine code?
items.answer: Your Peugeot 405 engine code is stamped on the engine block (visible near the gearbox or on a sticker in the engine bay) and can be instantly verified via DVLA lookup using your registration number on our platform.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block: near gearbox or on timing cover
Also on vehicle sticker: engine bay or driver's door shut
Common codes: XUD9 (diesel), XU7 (1.8L petrol), XU9J4 (Mi16)
Use our free DVLA registration lookup for instant verification
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot 405 engine code instantly via registration
